name: No Fry Doughnuts
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, dissolve yeast in warm water.
2. Add milk, sugar, salt, nutmeg, cinnamon, eggs, shortening, and 2 cups of flour to the yeast mixture. Mix on low speed for half a minute, scraping the bowl constantly.
3. Beat the mixture on medium speed for two minutes, scraping the bowl occasionally.
4. Stir in the remaining flour until the dough is smooth, scraping the side of the bowl.
5. Cover and let the dough rise in a warm place until it doubles in size, about 50-60 minutes.
6. Turn the dough onto a well-floured cloth-covered board and roll it out lightly to coat with flour. The dough will be soft to handle.
7. With a floured rolling pin, gently roll the dough to about 1/2-inch thick (or pat it gently with your hand). Cut into doughnuts using floured 2 1/2 inch doughnut cutters.
8. Lift the doughnuts carefully with a spatula and place them 2-inches apart on a greased baking sheet. Brush the doughnuts with melted butter. Cover and let rise until doubled, about 20 minutes.
9. Preheat the oven to 425°F (220°C).
10. Bake for 8-10 minutes or until golden brown. Immediately brush with melted butter and shake in cinnamon sugar, or if desired, spread with creamy glaze or chocolate glaze. Makes 1 dozen.
CHOCOLATE DOUGHNUTS: Omit nutmeg and cinnamon. Stir in 1/2 cup cocoa with the first addition of flour. After baking, shake doughnuts in confectioners sugar or spread with chocolate glaze.
APPLESAUCE DOUGHNUTS: Substitute 1 1/2 cups lukewarm applesauce for the milk. Substitute 1 teaspoon cinnamon, 1/2 teaspoon nutmeg, and 1/2 teaspoon cloves for the 1 teaspoon nutmeg and 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon.
